title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Automation and… · Language Clinic: Contract Changed Case Tests
summary: A data client needs injected openers, local HTTP fixtures, fake clocks, malformed and partial bodies, contract checks, and privacy-safe telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py contract changed case tests increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
